title: PMI KC localhost interactive application audit
purpose: Measure whether the locally rendered application actually behaves as documented, record evidence during the click-through, and produce implementation-ready recommendations without changing product code.

audit_contract.primary_checklist: This JSON file is the checklist and live audit record. Update it during the audit after each step or small group of steps, not only at the end.
audit_contract.source_guide_role: Historical source used to build this checklist. It is not the execution target and its production deployment steps are excluded from this localhost audit.

non_negotiable_safety: A localhost URL is required for every browser interaction in this audit.

Localhost is not proof that the backing data stores are isolated. Verify the active data mode before clicking any control that can persist, send, approve, assign, change access, create a draft, or write externally.

Use local demo records, test fixtures, or emulators for mutating tests. If a mutating control is backed by production or its isolation cannot be proven, do not click it; mark the step blocked_safety and document what remains unverified.
Use only a pmikcmetro.com identity. The personal josiah.abernathy@gmail.com account must never appear in any authentication path.
Do not send email. Do not execute a RentVine, Google Sheets, QuickBooks, banking, Gmail-send, or other system-of-record write.

Do not expose credentials, customer data, raw Gmail content, addresses, proposed values, free-text decision reasons, or customer records in the audit file, screenshots, terminal output, or final report.
Do not weaken permissions, approval gates, source-state warnings, identity boundaries, or the approximately $10 cloud budget cap.
Do not modify application code, tests, configuration, infrastructure, or production data. Only this audit record and gitignored evidence artifacts may be changed.
Do not commit, push, merge, deploy, reset the user's worktree, or discard existing changes.

Optional compose-only Gmail draft creation is excluded from this localhost audit unless a disposable approved mailbox and explicit per-action authorization are already documented. A visible draft-preview workflow can still be tested locally.

status_definitions.not_run: No interactive attempt has been made.
status_definitions.in_progress: The step has started but does not yet have a defensible result.
status_definitions.pass: The expected behavior was directly observed through the rendered localhost application.
status_definitions.fail: The rendered localhost application was clicked through and did not meet the expected behavior.
status_definitions.blocked_auth: A required approved authentication surface could not be completed.
status_definitions.blocked_safety: The step would risk a live mutation, send, external write, privacy exposure, or other forbidden action.
status_definitions.blocked_data: The required safe test record, role, source, or fixture was unavailable.
status_definitions.not_applicable: Repository evidence establishes that the criterion does not apply to this build; the reason must be recorded.

run_record.git_commit_tested: e4541866dd42fa4f30f8c4e0f38461d0ec5d753b (the audit began at eeefc75b5ee5ae38c7d5477458b79f01c4d76af3; HEAD advanced to the merge commit mid-run, and both commits have the identical tree aece46db9b5fb323db5ac4fa616c8f029975e310)
run_record.working_tree_state_before_audit: Clean on branch codex/add-local-audit-checklist tracking origin/codex/add-local-audit-checklist; no staged or unstaged changes were present before the audit record was updated.
run_record.authenticated_end_user_domain: pmikcmetro.com via documented local demo session (Admin); Firebase end-user auth was not used
run_record.data_isolation_mode: Mixed and partially breached: the running Next.js app inherited FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST from .env.local and its app-plane Firestore mutations were observed in the local 127.0.0.1:8080 emulator; LOCAL_DEMO_AUTH=true, MODEL_PROVIDER=local, and development speech used the stub. However, the shell environment did not export FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST, and scripts/seed-demo-records.mjs reads .env.local only for project IDs without exporting the emulator host. Therefore the earlier npm run seed:demo likely wrote the reported synthetic fixtures to the configured live Firestore project. No cleanup was attempted. ASK_DEMO_MODE=false and IMAGE_STORE=drive also left live-source/photo paths unisolated and unclicked.
run_record.summary_counting_rule: Count only test_cases[].steps. Authentication and startup steps are reported separately, and test-case result objects are aggregates rather than additional criteria.

run_notes: CRITICAL SAFETY INCIDENT: npm run seed:demo reported creating synthetic demo records while the shell FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST was unset. Source inspection shows the seed/reset modules do not propagate the .env.local emulator host or refuse production. The app-visible emulator queue remained empty. The command therefore likely wrote synthetic fixtures to the configured live Firestore project. No deletion or cleanup was attempted.

HEAD advanced during the audit from eeefc75b5ee5ae38c7d5477458b79f01c4d76af3 to merge commit e4541866dd42fa4f30f8c4e0f38461d0ec5d753b. The two commits have the identical Git tree, so the tested code did not change.

The in-app browser screenshot command repeatedly timed out; no screenshot file was successfully captured. Evidence entries are rendered DOM observations, interaction outcomes, local request logs, and bounded layout measurements.

findings.title: Dictate starts capture without explicit status and silently inserts the transcript
findings.observed_behavior: Clicking Dictate immediately changed the button to Stop recording and began capture. The application displayed no permission disclosure, recording status, timer, or live-region message. After Stop recording, the control returned to Dictate and the development stub transcript appeared in the Question field without a visible processing or success message.
findings.expected_behavior: The rendered workflow should make permission/capture intent explicit and expose distinct, accessible recording, processing, success, and failure states while preserving the non-sensitive local transcription boundary.
reproduction_steps: Start the localhost app with the documented local demo configuration and sign in as Admin.
Open /ask at 1440x1000.
Click Dictate once and observe the button and surrounding status area.
Click Stop recording and observe the transition and Question field.
evidence: Rendered DOM changed only from Dictate to Stop recording [pressed] during capture.
After stopping, the Question value became the development STT stub transcript with no visible processing or success message.
Relevant implementation: components/ask/AskForm.tsx; components/hooks/useAudioRecorder.ts; lib/speech/stt-provider.ts.
findings.user_impact: Users cannot confidently tell when audio capture begins, whether transcription is processing, or whether insertion succeeded; screen-reader users receive especially weak state feedback.
findings.safety_impact: Immediate capture without app-level disclosure increases the chance of unintended ambient recording, even though this audit used the local stub boundary.

implementation_recommendation.change_summary: Add an explicit pre-capture hint and a single aria-live status model for permission request, recording, transcribing, success, no-speech, and failure states without changing the existing provider or safety fence.
behavioral_requirements: Before capture, state that microphone access will be requested and audio is used only to fill the current Question field.
On start, announce Recording and expose a visible state independent of button focus.
On stop, show Transcribing until the request settles and then announce whether text was inserted.
Retain the existing permission-denied, unsupported-browser, no-speech, and network-error recovery copy.
Never submit the Ask form automatically after transcription.
data_or_api_contract_changes: No API shape change is required; expose recorder lifecycle status from useAudioRecorder or map existing callbacks into a typed UI-state enum.
permission_and_safety_constraints: Do not auto-request microphone permission before an explicit user gesture.
Do not persist audio or auto-submit the transcribed question.
Keep production STT and development stub selection behind the existing NODE_ENV fence.
edge_cases_to_handle: User navigates away while recording or transcribing
tests_to_add_or_update: Update AskForm tests to assert the visible and aria-live lifecycle states.
Add recorder-hook tests for permission denial, start, stop, auto-stop, and repeated-click idempotence.
Add a browser acceptance test that records a short local-stub sample and verifies Recording -> Transcribing -> transcript inserted without form submission.
acceptance_checks: At /ask as Admin, Dictate shows a pre-capture explanation before the first click.
During capture, visible text and an aria-live region announce Recording while Stop recording remains keyboard reachable.
After stop, Transcribing is visible until completion, followed by a success or actionable error message.
The transcript populates Question but Get answer is not invoked automatically.
Denied permission and unsupported-browser states provide a Type instead recovery path on desktop and phone.

findings.title: High-severity resolution depends on native window.confirm and cannot complete in the in-app browser
findings.observed_behavior: Required-field validation correctly blocked an empty High resolution. After a sanitized reason was entered, Resolve invoked window.confirm. The in-app browser auto-dismissed that native dialog before acceptance, so no High resolution persisted after multiple fresh-tab attempts and refreshes.
findings.expected_behavior: A High or Blocked resolution should require a plain-English reason and an explicit, accessible confirmation that can be completed consistently in supported browsers; acceptance should append exactly one audit record and cancellation should append none.
reproduction_steps: Sign in to localhost as local-demo Admin with Firestore pointed at the emulator.
Open the synthetic renewal run and View all flags.
On an unresolved High card, click Resolve with no reason and observe required-field blocking.
Enter a sanitized reason and click Resolve.
Attempt to accept the native confirmation, then refresh and inspect the card and resolved count.
evidence: The empty reason remained valueMissing and no resolution was created.
A populated valid reason followed by Resolve produced no persisted High resolution after refresh in the Codex in-app browser.
components/lease-renewal/flag-actions.tsx calls window.confirm for High-severity submissions.
findings.user_impact: High-risk decisions may be impossible to complete in embedded, automation-controlled, or dialog-restrictive browser contexts, despite valid input.
findings.safety_impact: The confirmation gate is appropriate and must remain, but an unreliable native dialog can create false confidence that a decision was saved or pressure users to retry.

implementation_recommendation.change_summary: Replace window.confirm with an app-rendered accessible confirmation dialog that preserves the High/Blocked gate and submits only after an explicit Continue action.
behavioral_requirements: Keep required plain-English reason validation before confirmation.
Render the confirmation in the application DOM with Cancel and Continue actions.
Continue must submit at most once and display a saving state until the API response settles.
Cancel must preserve entered form data and create no resolution.
Success must update the resolved count and append-only audit state without requiring a refresh.
files_or_components_to_inspect: styles/globals.css or the existing dialog component system
data_or_api_contract_changes: No route schema change is required; keep the existing resolution payload and append-only persistence contract.
permission_and_safety_constraints: Do not weaken the Admin/Approver capability checks or High/Blocked reason requirement.
Do not cause any Sheet or RentVine write; resolution remains app-plane only.
Keep confirmation explicit and non-bypassable by repeated clicks or keyboard shortcuts.
edge_cases_to_handle: Double-click or repeated Enter/Space on Continue
Back or route change with the dialog open
Phone viewport and long reason text
Focus restoration after Cancel or success
tests_to_add_or_update: Component test for empty-reason blocking before the dialog opens.
Component test for Cancel preserving input and making zero API calls.
Component test for Continue making exactly one API call and updating success state.
Browser test in the in-app/embedded Chromium surface at phone and desktop widths.
acceptance_checks: Empty High reason cannot open the confirmation dialog.
Valid High reason opens a labeled DOM dialog with focus trapped and Cancel/Continue keyboard reachable.
Cancel creates no audit record and returns focus to Resolve.
Continue creates exactly one resolution, updates the resolved count immediately, and preserves the reason in history.
Refresh shows the same single resolution; RentVine and operating Sheet remain unchanged.

findings.title: Notifications declares all caught up while Console still has decisions
findings.observed_behavior: The Console showed 4 Needs your decision items. The Notifications hub, opened through the rendered notification menu, omitted that decision signal and stated that the user was all caught up with nothing needing attention.
findings.expected_behavior: Notifications must be a value-free superset of the Console attention signals and must show the same decision count/source; empty event-log copy must not claim global all-clear while standing decisions remain.
reproduction_steps: Sign in to localhost as local-demo Admin with the synthetic approval and renewal fixtures loaded.
Open Console and note the Needs your decision count.
Open the Notifications menu and click See all notifications.

If GET /api/notifications serves the full hub, keep the additive field backward compatible.
permission_and_safety_constraints: Respect renewal Space scope and existing read capability before gathering decisions.
Keep summary and rows value-free through the existing attention whitelist.

findings.title: Hard-excluded Gmail drafting is bypassed by trivial category rephrasing
findings.observed_behavior: The exact category Legal/notices refused before the model. Changing only the free-text category to legal notice caused the request to enter Composing and produced a model-tailored draft containing the excluded legal request, proving that excluded content reached the model.
findings.expected_behavior: Legal/notices, Owner money, and Tenant disputes must fail closed before any model call under obvious casing, punctuation, singular/plural, whitespace, or equivalent category rephrasing; pasted excluded intent must not be drafted under a benign category.
reproduction_steps: Open /gmail-hub as an authorized local-demo user.
In Anticipatory draft, leave an Approved reply pattern selected.
Set Category to Legal/notices and enter the sanitized excluded-category test text; click Compose draft and observe refusal.
Change only Category to legal notice and click Compose draft again.
Observe Composing followed by a model-tailored draft containing the excluded request.
evidence: Exact Legal/notices rendered Refused before the model.
Rephrased legal notice rendered Model-tailored draft - review before sending.
lib/gmail-inbox-zero/drafts.ts checks GMAIL_HARD_EXCLUSION_CATEGORIES.includes(category) with exact display strings.
findings.user_impact: Users can generate drafts for categories the product explicitly promises will remain label-only, making the safety claim unreliable.
findings.safety_impact: Excluded legal/rights-related content reaches the model before human review, violating the deterministic pre-model safety boundary even though send authority remains preserved.

implementation_recommendation.change_summary: Replace free-text/exact-display category gating with a closed canonical category contract and a deterministic, server-side fail-closed normalization and excluded-intent preflight over category, subject, and pasted facts before model construction or invocation.
behavioral_requirements: Represent categories with stable IDs, not display strings, across UI, route schema, and deterministic spine.
Reject unknown category IDs instead of treating them as draftable.
Normalize case, Unicode, whitespace, punctuation, slash/hyphen variation, and singular/plural aliases before exclusion checks.
Conservatively detect obvious excluded intent in subject and pasted facts even if a benign category is selected.
Set refusedBeforeModel=true and prove model call count zero on every excluded or unknown case.
Keep the human-reviewed draft banner and no-Send boundary for allowed categories.
data_or_api_contract_changes: Change category from arbitrary string to a shared canonical enum/ID in the route and client state.
Return a typed fail-closed error for unknown, aliased-excluded, or content-detected excluded intent.
Do not send excluded text to createModelProvider or any model prompt.
permission_and_safety_constraints: The deterministic exclusion check must execute before model-provider construction and invocation.
Do not use the model itself to decide whether excluded text may reach the model.
Do not add Gmail read, draft creation, or send scope while fixing this app-plane text workflow.

owner money casing and punctuation variants
tenant dispute singular/plural and whitespace variants

findings.title: Demo fixture commands can target live Firestore when the emulator host exists only in .env.local
findings.route: localhost setup via npm run seed:demo
findings.role: authenticated owner CLI and ADC context
findings.observed_behavior: With FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST present in .env.local but absent from the PowerShell process environment, npm run seed:demo reported creating synthetic records. The running app's emulator-backed Approval Queue then contained zero general queue items. Source inspection shows the seed/reset modules read .env.local for project IDs but neither propagate nor require FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST before initializing Firebase Admin, so the command likely wrote the synthetic fixtures to the configured live Firestore project.
findings.expected_behavior: Demo seed, reset, and operator commands must fail closed before Firebase initialization unless their effective Firestore target is an explicitly allowed localhost emulator. They must print the non-sensitive target, never infer permission to write production from a project ID, and require a separately designed and owner-approved live workflow for any live mutation.
reproduction_steps: Configure .env.local with a production project identifier and FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST=127.0.0.1:8080.
Start the emulator and local app so the app runtime inherits .env.local.
From a new PowerShell process where $env:FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST is unset, run npm run seed:demo.
Observe that the command reports seeded records instead of refusing to run.
Inspect the app-visible emulator and observe that the expected general Approval Queue fixtures are absent.
evidence: PowerShell inspection after seeding: FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST=<unset>.
npm run seed:demo reported seeded synthetic SOP, template, placeholder, tool, and approval-queue records.
Rendered emulator-backed Approval Queue: 0 queue items and an empty All items view.
scripts/seed-demo-records.mjs and scripts/demo-firestore.mjs pass a project ID to Firebase Admin but do not set or validate the emulator host.
No cleanup or deletion was attempted after discovery.
findings.user_impact: A routine local QA setup command can silently mutate the client production database, contaminate operational views, and undermine confidence in all local-fixture workflows.
findings.safety_impact: This is a production-write isolation breach. Although the emitted fixtures are synthetic, the command crossed the repository's system-of-record and live-write approval gates without an explicit live flag or owner authorization.

implementation_recommendation.change_summary: Centralize a fail-closed demo Firestore target guard and require every seed/reset/operator entry point to establish and verify a localhost emulator before Firebase Admin is initialized.
behavioral_requirements: Resolve the effective emulator host once, using an allowlisted local value and never merely a project ID.
Refuse to run when FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST is absent, malformed, non-local, or the emulator is unreachable.
Print the effective non-secret host, port, and demo project before any write.
Make seed and reset idempotent inside the emulator and impossible against production by default.
Keep any future live maintenance command separate, explicitly named, owner-approved, and governed by the Action Registry.
data_or_api_contract_changes: No product API contract change is required.
The CLI contract should require a verified localhost emulator target and expose that target in counts-only startup output.
permission_and_safety_constraints: Do not automatically delete or modify the suspected live fixtures as part of the fix.
Do not use a personal account or alternate project as a workaround.
Do not add a permissive --live switch to demo seed/reset commands.
Preserve the no-customer-data-in-git and no-secrets-in-output rules.
edge_cases_to_handle: Host present only in the parent shell
Production project ID paired with a local emulator
IPv4 localhost, localhost name, and approved IPv6 loopback forms
tests_to_add_or_update: Child-process test: absent shell emulator host fails before Firebase initialization even when .env.local contains production project metadata.
Target-guard unit tests for local allowlist, malformed host, non-local host, and unreachable emulator.
Integration test: verified 127.0.0.1 emulator accepts seed and the fixtures appear only there.
Reset and demo-operator tests proving they preserve and validate the guarded target.
Regression test that production Firebase Admin initialization is never reached from demo seed/reset entry points.
acceptance_checks: With FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST unset or non-local, npm run seed:demo exits nonzero before any write and names the required corrective action.
With a verified local emulator, npm run seed:demo writes the expected fixtures to the emulator and prints its target.
Stopping the emulator causes seed/reset to fail before writes rather than falling back to production.
The configured production Firestore remains unchanged during all demo seed/reset tests.
An owner-reviewed incident check determines whether the suspected synthetic live records require a separately authorized cleanup.

dependencies_or_blockers: Owner review is required to inspect the production project and decide whether the suspected synthetic records should be removed through an authorized, narrowly scoped cleanup.

The default decision inbox and the open alternate-view panel both expanded to the oversized grid track, requiring horizontal scrolling.

reproduction_steps: Open /approval-queue through the localhost primary navigation.
Set the browser viewport to 390x844.

Long-label fixture test for inbox, renewal-review, and write-back rows.
Component/CSS regression check that the tab strip can scroll locally without widening the document.
acceptance_checks: At 390x844, the collapsed queue and every expanded tab have document scrollWidth equal to clientWidth.
Long labels wrap without overlapping the High or Blocked chip.

findings.title: Maintenance permits ticket creation while required-input blockers remain
findings.observed_behavior: Building a draft with no issue description and no selected unit rendered two clear Before submitting blockers, but the Create ticket button remained enabled. The client sends that blocked draft unchanged, and the server creation schema accepts description as an empty string and unit as null. The audit did not click Create ticket or persist a record.
findings.expected_behavior: A blocker-bearing draft must not be persistable as a tracked ticket. The Create ticket control should remain disabled with an accessible explanation until an issue exists and a unit suggestion has been explicitly selected; the server must independently reject incomplete input.
reproduction_steps: Open Maintenance Work Order Intake from Spaces.
Leave Issue empty and do not select a unit suggestion.
Click Build work-order draft; this is a client-only preview action.
Observe both missing-input blockers in the preview.
Observe that Create ticket is still enabled; do not click it against live or uncertain data.
evidence: Rendered blockers: Add an issue description or voice note; Match the location to a unit.
Rendered Create ticket button was enabled in the same preview.
MaintenanceCapture.createTicket posts draft.description and draft.unit without checking draft.blockers.
CreateMaintenanceTicketInputSchema defaults description to an empty string and unit to null.
findings.user_impact: Operators can create noisy, unactionable tickets lacking both the reported problem and a verified location, forcing manual cleanup and weakening queue trust.
findings.safety_impact: The write remains inside the app plane and does not create a RentVine work order, but incomplete records can generate audit activity and notification noise and may later be mistaken for execution-ready work.

implementation_recommendation.change_summary: Enforce required issue and verified-unit invariants in both the Maintenance UI and ticket API before any app-plane ticket write.
behavioral_requirements: Disable Create ticket whenever draft.blockers is non-empty and associate the control with visible blocker guidance.
Require a non-empty description and a non-null verified unit in the server input schema or a dedicated ticket-readiness assertion.
Preserve the ability to preview a blocked draft so missing inputs remain visible.
If a selected unit is edited or cleared, invalidate the selection and disable creation immediately.
On a stale or crafted POST, return a specific 400 response without creating ticket, activity, or notification records.
data_or_api_contract_changes: Tighten CreateMaintenanceTicketInputSchema so description is trimmed and non-empty and unit is a required verified object.
Return a stable validation error shape for blocker-bearing or incomplete ticket creation requests.
permission_and_safety_constraints: Keep ticket creation app-plane only and preserve the human approval gate before any RentVine write.
Do not silently auto-select a unit from raw typeahead text.
Do not persist a partial ticket merely to preserve form progress; use client state or a separately specified draft record if needed.
edge_cases_to_handle: Voice transcript present with no typed note
Exact unit text typed but suggestion not selected
Stale client against tightened API validation
tests_to_add_or_update: Component test: blocker-bearing draft renders disabled Create ticket and guidance.
Component test: typed exact label without selecting remains blocked.
Component test: valid description plus selected synthetic unit enables exactly one create request.
Route/schema tests rejecting empty description and null unit before Firestore is called.
Persistence test confirming rejected requests create no ticket, activity, or notification documents.
acceptance_checks: Empty or unmatched drafts cannot invoke POST /api/maintenance/tickets.
A crafted incomplete POST returns 400 and creates zero emulator documents.
Selecting a verified synthetic unit and adding an issue enables Create ticket.
One valid click creates one emulator-backed ticket while RentVine remains untouched.
Clearing or editing the selected unit disables creation again.

findings.title: Property decision history is orphaned from renewal records and runs
findings.observed_behavior: A synthetic renewal record and the reconciliation run exposed workflow details, evidence, flag resolution, and flag-scoped activity but no rendered link to Property decision history. The repository contains an Admin-only dynamic history page, yet no application component links to its route, making it undiscoverable without constructing a property key manually.
findings.expected_behavior: An authorized user should be able to open property-scoped decision history from a renewal record or reconciliation run using a clearly named link. The link must carry the canonical property key internally, preserve scope and Admin checks, and never require users to infer or type dynamic route identifiers.
reproduction_steps: Open Spaces, then Lease Renewals.
Open a synthetic renewal record from Needs your attention.
Inspect the record sections and actions for a decision-history destination.
Open Approval Queue and navigate to the synthetic reconciliation run.
Inspect the run summary, flags, evidence links, resolved disclosure, and activity; observe that no property-history link exists.
evidence: Renewal record exposed no history link.
Reconciliation run exposed flag-scoped Decision history but no property-scoped link.
Repository search found the Property decision history page but no href to /lease-renewal/property/[propertyKey].
The audit did not bypass the missing UI by constructing the route.
findings.user_impact: Admins cannot discover the cross-run property audit trail from the work they are reviewing, so prior decisions may be missed and the existing history feature provides no practical value.
findings.safety_impact: The authorization boundary remains intact, but an inaccessible audit trail makes it harder to verify who decided what before later approval or write-back authorization.

implementation_recommendation.change_summary: Add authorized, canonical property-history links to the renewal record and unambiguous reconciliation-run contexts, with an honest unavailable state when no property key can be derived.
behavioral_requirements: Render View decision history on renewal records that have a canonical property key.
Render the same destination from a reconciliation flag only when its source trigger maps unambiguously to one property.
Omit or disable the destination with specific copy for name-joined or ambiguous flags.
Keep the destination Admin-only and preserve renewals space checks.
Return users to the originating record or run, not only the generic Renewals list.
data_or_api_contract_changes: Expose or derive the canonical property key in the client-safe record/run view model without leaking addresses into value-free summary surfaces.
Optionally carry an origin query parameter or route state for the Back destination.
permission_and_safety_constraints: Retain requirePageSpaceAccess(renewals) and manageAdmin enforcement on property history.
Never construct a link for ambiguous source-trigger-to-property mappings.
Do not expose property keys, addresses, reasons, or values in global summaries or notifications.
tests_to_add_or_update: Component test: canonical property record renders a correctly encoded history link.
Component test: ambiguous or missing property mapping renders no unsafe link and explains why.
Authorization tests for Admin, scoped non-Admin, and out-of-scope users.
Browser test navigating record to history and back across empty and populated fixtures.
Responsive test for long reasons and multiple entries.
acceptance_checks: From a synthetic renewal record, View decision history opens the correct property history without manually editing the URL.

No-history and unavailable states are explicit.

Back returns to the originating renewal context on desktop and phone.

findings.title: Maintenance photo upload bypasses its closed Action Registry gate
findings.observed_behavior: Maintenance exposed Add / take photo with no target preview or gate warning. In the audited localhost configuration, IMAGE_STORE resolved to drive and the non-secret folder/DWD configuration names were populated. Selecting a file would POST to a route that directly creates the Drive-backed image store and calls put(); it never checks the Action Registry entry, which is Needs Permission and production_allowed:false. No file was selected and no upload was attempted.
findings.expected_behavior: The upload control and route must fail closed while google_drive.maintenance_photo.store is not executable. Once a future owner-approved gate opens, the UI must preview filename, MIME type, and target in-boundary folder, require the authorized user action, and enforce the same registry decision server-side before uploading.
reproduction_steps: Start localhost with IMAGE_STORE=drive and the maintenance Drive/DWD configuration present.
Open Spaces, then Maintenance Work Order Intake.
Observe the Add / take photo control and the absence of a target preview or closed-gate message; do not select a file.
Reconcile google_drive.maintenance_photo.store in the Action Registry and observe Needs Permission with production_allowed:false.
Inspect POST /api/maintenance/photo and observe that it calls the configured store without registry enforcement.
evidence: Rendered control: Add / take photo; no Drive target, preview, approval, or unavailable message.
Non-secret environment posture: IMAGE_STORE, maintenance folder, DWD service-account, and DWD subject configuration were present.
Action Registry entry google_drive.maintenance_photo.store: readiness Needs Permission; production_allowed false.
The registry preview schema requires filename, MIME type, and target folder before upload.
app/api/maintenance/photo/route.ts does not call isActionExecutable and immediately invokes store.put after payload validation.
findings.user_impact: A field operator can unknowingly create a live client-Drive file from what appears to be a normal local capture control, without seeing where it will be stored or whether the action is approved.
findings.safety_impact: This bypasses the central external-action readiness gate and can write customer media to a live client Drive folder despite production_allowed:false. It also defeats the localhost audit's non-live isolation expectation.

implementation_recommendation.change_summary: Enforce the Drive photo Action Registry gate in both the rendered control and the upload route, and add the registry-defined preview before any permitted external write.
behavioral_requirements: Hide or disable Add / take photo with explicit unavailable copy while the action is not executable.
Check isActionExecutable or the canonical equivalent server-side before reading or storing the payload.
When the gate eventually opens, render filename, MIME type, and a safe target-folder label before the user confirms upload.
Return a typed closed-gate response without constructing a Drive client or uploading bytes.
Force the stub store for local-demo sessions unless an explicit, separately approved live-upload test protocol is active.
data_or_api_contract_changes: Add a safe readiness/preview response that exposes only action status and a non-sensitive folder label, never credentials or a raw customer path.
Use a stable needs-permission error type for POST attempts while the registry gate is closed.
permission_and_safety_constraints: Do not open production_allowed as part of this defect fix.
Do not upload a test file to prove the closed gate; assert that no Drive client/write occurs.
Preserve pmikcmetro.com DWD identity and the in-boundary folder requirement for any future approved execution.
Never print folder IDs, credentials, customer images, or image bytes in logs or audit output.
edge_cases_to_handle: Gate changes between preview and upload
tests_to_add_or_update: Route test: closed registry gate returns typed refusal before createMaintenanceImageStore or put is called.
Component test: closed gate renders unavailable copy and no actionable file input.
Local-demo configuration test forcing stub or refusing Drive without explicit approved protocol.
Preview contract tests for filename, MIME type, and safe folder label.
Future open-gate integration test with an injected fake store proving one confirmed upload and no duplicate.
acceptance_checks: With production_allowed:false, /maintenance exposes no live upload action and POST creates zero Drive files.
A crafted POST while closed returns the stable gate error before any Drive API call.
Local demo cannot resolve to a live Drive store by ordinary .env.local configuration alone.
When separately approved and opened, the user sees the registry-required preview before one upload.
RentVine, email-send, and other external boundaries remain unchanged.

dependencies_or_blockers: The action must remain closed until the required Drive permission and owner-approved execution protocol are documented in the Action Registry.

final_report_requirements: State the exact localhost URL, commit, pre-existing worktree state, authenticated role, tested viewports, and proven data-isolation mode.
List the authentication steps completed and distinguish CLI or ADC authentication from Firebase end-user and Firebase CLI authentication.
Summarize automated checks separately from browser-click evidence.
Give pass, fail, blocked_auth, blocked_safety, blocked_data, not_applicable, and not_run counts derived from this JSON.
For each advertised area, state whether the criteria were directly met, directly failed, or could not be proven safely.

Rank findings by critical, high, medium, and low severity and separate functional defects, UX or copy improvements, accessibility concerns, documentation mismatches, and correctly enforced safety boundaries.

Make every recommended change specific enough for an outside implementation model to edit comprehensively without repeating discovery: exact observed and expected behavior, reproduction steps, likely code areas, behavioral requirements, safety constraints, edge cases, tests, and click-through acceptance checks.

final_report.overall_assessment: Not release-ready. The localhost application was authenticated and exercised across all advertised areas, but the audit found one critical fixture-isolation breach, three high-severity safety/attention defects, seven medium defects, and a failed isolation startup gate. The most urgent action is owner-led incident review of the likely synthetic live-Firestore writes; no cleanup was attempted. Product remediation should then close the demo Firestore guard, Gmail category normalization, Maintenance Drive Action Registry enforcement, and Notifications false-all-clear behavior before broader interactive testing. No product code, production data, deployment, commit, or external send was intentionally changed by this audit.

criteria_summary.startup_and_authentication: 6 passed, ISOLATION-01 failed, and Firebase CLI authentication was not applicable. CLI/ADC used josiah@pmikcmetro.com; the rendered app used documented local-demo Admin auth, not Firebase end-user auth.

areas.summary: Shell navigation and recovery passed; live-backed Ask was safely blocked and Dictate lifecycle feedback failed.
Synthetic resolution, authorization separation, skip, and phone decider worked; live review was safely blocked and High resolution failed at native confirmation.
Rendered role/scope separation passed, but no live teammate claims were mutated and no disposable scoped Firebase Auth user existed.
Coverage and value-free standing summaries worked; Notifications falsely declared all caught up and unread/digest fixtures were absent.
Read gating, structured preview, no-Send boundary, and exact exclusion worked; a rephrased exclusion bypassed the gate and phone layout overflowed.
Families, waiting states, and no-schedule copy worked; a missing process definition still rendered a start action that returned 404.
Urgent-first default and full-surface boundaries worked; safe inline fixtures were absent and expanded phone views overflowed.
Synthetic unit matching, refresh/Back, phone layout, and RentVine/no-send copy worked; incomplete tickets remained creatable and the photo route bypassed a closed registry gate.
Property history was unreachable from rendered renewal records/runs, so its empty, populated, long-content, and phone states were unproven.
Semantic structure, visible focus, recovery copy, and most responsive flows were sound; coherence, lifecycle feedback, failure-prone actions, and two phone overflow cases failed.
Human send authority, renewal writeback separation, value-free summaries, and visible uncertainty held; the Maintenance Drive action did not honor its registry gate.

Typecheck and 51 targeted tests passed; lint completed with no errors.

recommended_changes_in_priority_order.change: Fail-close every demo seed/reset/operator entry point on a verified local emulator and conduct an owner-authorized incident review of the suspected synthetic production records.
Replace free-text exact display-label exclusion checks with canonical category IDs, normalized aliases, and a fail-closed subject/facts scan before any model call.
Enforce google_drive.maintenance_photo.store readiness in both UI and route, keep it closed, and require the registry-defined preview before any future approved upload.
Use the same full needs-decision gatherer for Notifications and Console so a nonzero decision backlog can never coexist with an all-clear message.
Disable and server-reject maintenance ticket creation until an issue and explicitly selected verified unit satisfy all draft blockers.
Reconcile anticipated process IDs against loaded definitions and render Open the space instead of a start button when the definition is absent.
Replace native window.confirm with an accessible in-app confirmation dialog that preserves the High-severity gate and supports reliable recovery.
Link authorized, unambiguous renewal records and run flags to property decision history and preserve a safe Back destination.
Constrain Gmail Hub grid/form children and long selects to the phone viewport without hiding content globally.
Set min-width:0 and wrapping rules on Approval Queue grid/flex children while keeping only the tab strip locally scrollable.
Add explicit accessible recording, processing, success, empty, and error lifecycle feedback to Dictate.

correctly_enforced_boundaries: No personal Google identity appeared; CLI/ADC authentication used the pmikcmetro.com account.
Firebase end-user authentication and Firebase CLI authentication were kept distinct and were not invoked unnecessarily.
No email Send control appeared and no email was sent or scheduled.
Gmail mailbox read remained explicitly disabled in the Hub.
Renewal resolution, write-back authorization, and actual Sheet/RentVine execution remained separate states.
Live Ask, live renewal review, live role/scope mutation, and live photo upload were not clicked when privacy or mutation isolation was not proven.
Value-free global summaries omitted addresses, values, and free-text reasons.
Blocked renewal conflicts and unmatched maintenance units surfaced uncertainty instead of inventing a value or match.

blocked_or_unproven_criteria.reason: Ask and live renewal review could expose configured client sources; synthetic supporting flows were used instead.
Scope change/restore would mutate live Firebase Auth claims for real teammates.
No disposable approved-domain scoped Firebase Auth user existed for denial and phone checks.
No isolated unread event or qualifying team-review digest fixture rendered.
The emulator had no eligible Low/Medium general row, self-assigned row, or awaiting-writeback fixture; no new mutation was manufactured after the isolation incident.
Zero tickets meant no assignment picker or approved-user roster was reachable.
The missing rendered navigation path prevented safe access to empty, populated, long-reason, refresh, and phone history states.

documentation_mismatches.mismatch: Local demo guidance presents seed/reset as emulator work, but the scripts do not propagate or require the .env.local emulator host and can initialize production Admin Firestore.
Notifications presents itself as the attention hub/all-clear source while omitting the decision gatherer that powers Console.
Gmail Hub describes categories as hard-excluded, but enforcement compares exact free-text display labels and is bypassed by equivalent wording.
Anticipated work promises a one-click test run for supported families even when the corresponding process definition is absent.

The Action Registry keeps Maintenance Drive photo storage at Needs Permission/production_allowed:false with a required preview, while the route exposes and can execute the upload without that gate or preview.
Property decision history exists as an Admin feature but no renewal record or run provides the advertised user navigation path.
